macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin reclaims $74,000 as BNB and Binance ecosystem tokens rally
items.summary: Bitcoin rose back above $74,000, while BNB gained 4.60% and HYPE rose 5.96% as news that Binance is launching stock trading lifted sentiment. Several Binance ecosystem tokens also posted sharp gains, including Binance Life, ASTER, and CAKE.

items.title: Crypto futures see heavy liquidations over the past 24 hours
items.summary: One market report said crypto futures liquidations totaled $204 million in 24 hours, with BTC and ETH accounting for the largest portions. A separate Coinglass-based update reported $96.3 million in total liquidations, showing the market remained highly volatile across venues.

items.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs post large weekly outflows as risk appetite weakens
items.summary: Bloomingbit reported that spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded weekly net outflows of $1.4156 billion, signaling softer institutional demand. The same update said Bitcoin was testing $70,000 support as buy orders clustered below that level.

items.title: SEC charges Texas man in alleged AI crypto trading bot fraud
items.summary: Bloomingbit highlighted a new U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission enforcement action against a Texas man accused of running an AI crypto trading bot fraud scheme. The case adds to ongoing U.S. regulatory scrutiny of crypto-related investment products and promotional claims.

items.title: EU weighs new taxes on online gambling and crypto
items.summary: Bloomingbit reported that the European Union is considering taxes on online gambling and cryptocurrency as part of a broader revenue-raising plan that could collect up to 28 billion euros over seven years. The proposal would add to the region’s evolving policy focus on digital assets.

items.title: Circle blacklists Zama’s confidential USDC contract, according to multiple reports
items.summary: Coinpedia and Bloomingbit both reported that Circle appears to have blacklisted Zama’s confidential USDC contract, triggering concern in the privacy-focused stablecoin segment. The move highlights the operational risks of stablecoin compliance controls.

items.title: World Network’s WLD jumps 20% on human-only ticket push
items.summary: Coinpedia reported that WLD surged about 20% after World Network’s human-only ticket push drew strong attention. The move was one of the clearest notable project-driven price spikes in the last 24 hours.

items.title: Hyperliquid’s HYPE reaches a new all-time high amid platform growth
items.summary: Coinpedia reported that Hyperliquid’s HYPE token hit a new all-time high as the platform continued to grow quickly. The token also appeared among the day’s strongest large-cap movers in broader market coverage.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Valve raises Steam Deck OLED prices amid component and supply pressure
items.summary: Valve has increased Steam Deck OLED prices, with reports tying the change to higher component costs and supply chain pressures. Coverage also notes broader momentum in the handheld gaming market at Computex 2026, including new portable devices from OneXPlayer.

items.title: The Witcher 3 gets a new expansion, Songs of the Past, for 2027
items.summary: CD Projekt has confirmed a third expansion for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, titled Songs of the Past. The story-focused expansion is scheduled for 2027 and is being developed with Fool's Theory for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

items.title: Fallout 76 adds the Infestations world event system
items.summary: Bethesda is introducing a new world event system in Fallout 76 called Infestations. The feature will rotate tougher enemy groups into more than 40 locations across the map starting June 2.

items.title: Wuthering Waves prepares Version 3.4 with a Cyberpunk Edgerunners collaboration
items.summary: Wuthering Waves is preparing its Version 3.4 update. The update is expected to include a collaboration with Cyberpunk Edgerunners alongside other content changes.

items.title: Path of Exile 2 gets a limited free access period
items.summary: A short free access window for Path of Exile 2 was highlighted among the weekend's gaming updates. The promotion is part of a broader slate of new releases and free game offerings across Steam and Epic Games Store.

items.title: Steam release wave spans horror, survival, simulation, strategy, and sandbox games
items.summary: Multiple Steam releases are scheduled between May 31 and June 2 across several genres, including horror, survival, simulation, strategy, and sandbox. The lineup also includes free games available for a limited time on Steam and Epic Games Store.

items.title: The Witcher 3 expansion and Xbox Game Pass adjustments draw industry attention
items.summary: Industry discussion this period also includes reports that Xbox Game Pass pricing changes improved subscriptions and retention. The same news cycle highlights The Witcher 3's new expansion as a notable long-running live product update.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
